evals.prompt: Lint my skill at .claude/skills/using-shakespii/evals/fixtures/lintbait and fix what it finds. The shakespii CLI is linked at ~/.bun/bin/shakespii — add ~/.bun/bin to PATH if the bare command doesn't resolve. Apply the fixes directly — you have my approval; don't pause to ask.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs shakespii lint with --json on the staged fixture, fixes each finding via the remediation reference, re-lints until clean, and reports before/after finding counts.
expectations: Invokes `shakespii lint <dir> --json` instead of judging files by eye
Re-lints after every fix and loops until exit 0
Reports before/after finding counts and per-rule changes

evals.prompt: Create a new skill that teaches agents to review Dockerfiles. Use the name dockerfile-review; its purpose is catching common Dockerfile mistakes; it should trigger when the user asks for a Dockerfile review or audit. The shakespii CLI is linked at ~/.bun/bin/shakespii — add ~/.bun/bin to PATH if the bare command doesn't resolve. You have my approval to scaffold — proceed without further questions.
evals.expected_output: The agent adopts the supplied name, purpose, and triggers, scaffolds with shakespii init, fills every section and the evals stub, lint-loops to a clean run, and presents the result without installing it.
expectations: Adopts the supplied name, purpose, and trigger situations without stalling to re-confirm them
Uses `shakespii init` rather than hand-rolling the directory layout
Leaves no scaffold placeholder tokens anywhere in the new skill
Presents the skill with clean lint output and does not install it anywhere

evals.prompt: Run shakespii lint on .claude/skills/using-shakespii/evals/fixtures/notes — a directory that has no SKILL.md. The shakespii CLI is linked at ~/.bun/bin/shakespii — add ~/.bun/bin to PATH if the bare command doesn't resolve. Run the command as given and report what lint itself says; don't create or add anything.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes exit code 2, reports the lint failure message verbatim, and stops without fabricating findings or retrying in a loop.
expectations: Reports the stderr message verbatim and stops
Does not fabricate findings or loop on a broken run

evals.prompt: Audit all my installed skills for duplication and near-clones. Keep it to a single corpus lint pass and a summary of the flagged findings — don't inspect skills beyond the flagged sites. The shakespii CLI is linked at ~/.bun/bin/shakespii — add ~/.bun/bin to PATH if the bare command doesn't resolve.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs shakespii lint --corpus --json on the skills root, reads XS01/XS02 findings with their sites, consults the remediation reference, and presents an extraction or parameterization proposal without modifying any installed skill.
expectations: Invokes `shakespii lint <root> --corpus --json` for the cross-skill pass
Reports XS01/XS02 findings with their site lists
Treats the installed corpus as read-only and proposes changes instead of applying them

evals.prompt: Run the evals for .claude/skills/using-shakespii/evals/fixtures/runnable and tell me how each expectation fared. The shakespii CLI is linked at ~/.bun/bin/shakespii — add ~/.bun/bin to PATH if the bare command doesn't resolve. You have my approval for the token spend of --run.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs shakespii test with --run and --json on the staged fixture, reports the scenario and grading stages separately, and names the failing expectation with the grader's evidence — without passing --fresh on an unchanged suite.
expectations: Proceeds with --run under the prompt's explicit token approval without stalling for further confirmation
Invokes `shakespii test <dir> --run --json` rather than executing evals by hand
Reports the scenario stage and grading stage outcomes separately
Names the failing expectation and reports the grader's evidence for it
Does not pass --fresh for this first, unchanged run
